bugs.title: Usage counter undercounts by 1
bugs.description: Sent 4 SendMessage calls (1 selection + 2 planning + 1 companion) but /api/usage reports used=3. The selection-mode message may not be counted because no trip_id is set at send time, or there is an off-by-one in the usage tracking logic.

bugs.title: AI re-called create_itinerary_items with duplicate items on second planning message
bugs.description: When user said 'Add those to my itinerary please', the AI attempted to re-create all 18 itinerary items that were already added in the previous message. The backend correctly returned already_exists/skipped_count=18, but the AI should not have called the tool again with duplicate items. This wastes tokens and adds latency.
bugs.expected: AI recognizes items were already created and confirms without re-calling the tool
bugs.actual: AI called create_itinerary_items with all 18 duplicate items; backend rejected them gracefully

bugs.title: Companion mode AI unnecessarily called delete_itinerary_items and create_itinerary_items
bugs.description: In companion mode, the user asked 'Where should I eat dinner tonight near Alfama?' — a pure informational query. The AI tried to call delete_itinerary_items and create_itinerary_items to modify the itinerary. The backend correctly rejected both calls with status=not_needed, but the AI should not have attempted to modify the itinerary for a simple dinner recommendation question in companion mode.
bugs.expected: AI answers the question without calling itinerary modification tools
bugs.actual: AI called delete_itinerary_items and create_itinerary_items before answering; backend returned not_needed for both

ux_issues.description: ListChatSessions returns lastMessageAt instead of updated_at. The field naming is functional but inconsistent with typical CRUD patterns that use created_at/updated_at pairs.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider adding an updated_at field to session metadata for consistency, or document the use of lastMessageAt in the API docs.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI re-creates already-existing itinerary items when user says 'add those to my itinerary' even though items were already added in the previous turn
ai_behavior_issues.context: Planning mode, second message: 'Add those to my itinerary please.' AI called create_itinerary_items with all 18 items that were already created.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI tried to modify itinerary in companion mode for a simple informational query
ai_behavior_issues.context: Companion mode: 'I just arrived in Lisbon. Where should I eat dinner tonight near Alfama?' AI called delete_itinerary_items and create_itinerary_items before answering.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Chat history persistence and session management work correctly. All messages round-tripped accurately through Firestore — no empty messages, no missing content, correct role alternation, and complete cross-session isolation. The AI's tendency to re-call tools with duplicate items is wasteful but the backend handles it gracefully. Session listing, history retrieval, and timestamp ordering all function as expected.
